name: Transform POP
description: The Transform POP applies a translate, rotate and scale to the position (attribute P) and normal (attribute N) of all the points of the input. It can apply the transform to any float3 attribute, not just P, and then output to any existing or new float3 attribute, by default P. It can apply the transform to point, vertex or primitive attributes. It can apply partial transforms to the points based on a 0 to 1 Weight attribute so that some parts of the geometry are affected and other parts not transformed. The [[Field POP]] can generate a Weight attribute to be used by Transform. '''Per-point mapping of parameters''' - using the Map page, every point can receive a different value for the transform parameters, based on mapping incoming attributes to the translate, scale, rotate, pivot etc. parameters, So essentially every point can get a completely different transform. See [[Mapping POP Attributes to Parameters]]. A further transform can be applied by specifying a Look At component (typically a [[Null COMP]] or [[Geometry COMP]]), and choosing which axis points to the origin of that component. Another transform may be applied by providing a 16-value transform matrix via a CHOP or DAT. The order of applying the translate, rotate and scale parameters is determined by the Transform Order and the Rotate Order menus. The Inverse toggle parameter applies an inverse of the transform specified. (Instead of tx=2 ry=50 sz=4, the equivalent of sz=.25, ry=-50 tx=-2) On the Align page there are numerous options for aligning the X, Y or Z coordinate to the different sides/center of the input's bounding box or to the bounding box of a reference POP. See also [[Math Mix POP]], [[Trig POP]]

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.label: Mode
parameters.description: Choose what the transform is applied to.

parameters.name: Attribute Class
parameters.label: Attribute Class
parameters.description: Makes the POP operate on point attributes, vertex attributes or primitive attributes where applicable.

parameters.name: Input Attribute (Scope)
parameters.label: Input Attribute (Scope)
parameters.description: Input attribute when mode is Transform Attribute, input attribute scope when mode is Transform Attribute Scope

parameters.name: Group
parameters.label: Group
parameters.description: If there are input groups, specifying a group name in this field will cause this POP to act only upon the group specified.

parameters.name: Weight Attribute
parameters.label: Weight Attribute
parameters.description: Specifies the scope of the attribute that stores weight values

parameters.name: Transform Order
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: Sets the overall transform order for the transformations.

parameters.name: Rotate Order
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description: Sets the order of the rotations within the overall transform order.

parameters.name: Translate
parameters.label: Translate
parameters.description: Translate the points in the three axes.

parameters.name: Rotate
parameters.label: Rotate
parameters.description: Rotate the points around the corresponding X, Y and Z axes. Angles are given in degrees.

parameters.name: Scale
parameters.label: Scale
parameters.description: Scale factor for each axis.

parameters.name: Pivot
parameters.label: Pivot
parameters.description: The pivot point for the transform rotates and scales.

parameters.name: Uniform Scale
parameters.label: Uniform Scale
parameters.description: Specifies a uniform scale factor in all axes.

parameters.name: Invert
parameters.label: Invert
parameters.description: Invert the transform resulting from the Translate, Rotate and Scale parameters.

parameters.name: Vectors Maintain Length
parameters.label: Vectors Maintain Length
parameters.description: Enable preserving the original vector length after applying transformations.

parameters.name: Look At
parameters.label: Look At
parameters.description: Allows you to orient your object by naming the object you would like it to Look At, or (Z axis) point to. Once you have designated this object to look at, it will continue to face that object, even if you move it. This is useful if, for instance, you want a camera to follow another object's movements. The Look At parameter points the object in question at the other object's origin.

parameters.name: Up Vector
parameters.label: Up Vector
parameters.description: Sets the up vector when setting up the look at transform.

parameters.name: Forward Direction
parameters.label: Forward Direction
parameters.description: Determines the forward direction for look at transformation.

parameters.name: Xform Matrix/CHOP/DAT
parameters.label: Xform Matrix/CHOP/DAT
parameters.description: Sets as transform using a 4x4 matrix directly.

parameters.name: Multiply Order
parameters.label: Multiply Order
parameters.description: Order of transform between the transform matrix and the transform resulting from the Translate Rotate Scale parameters.

parameters.name: Output Attribute (Scope)
parameters.label: Output Attribute (Scope)
parameters.description: Name of attribute to output (can choose components of attribute), can choose from menu.

parameters.name: Align Transform Order
parameters.label: Align Transform Order
parameters.description: Whether the transform from the Transform page or the Align page is applied first.

parameters.name: Align Operation Order
parameters.label: Align Operation Order
parameters.description: Set the order in which scale and transform is applied in the align transform.

parameters.name: Align Translate X
parameters.label: Align Translate X
parameters.description: Sets the center of the geometry in X for the align transform.

parameters.name: From Input
parameters.label: From Input
parameters.description: Determines which part of the input geometry to align to the Origin or Reference Input as selected in Post Translate parameter above.

parameters.name: To Reference
parameters.label: To Reference
parameters.description: Determines where on the reference the input is aligned.

parameters.name: Align Translate Y
parameters.label: Align Translate Y
parameters.description: Sets the center of the geometry in Y for the align transform.

parameters.name: From Input
parameters.label: From Input
parameters.description: Determines which part of the input geometry to align to the Origin or Reference Input as selected in Post Translate parameter above.

parameters.name: To Reference
parameters.label: To Reference
parameters.description: Determines where on the reference the input is aligned.

parameters.name: Align Translate Z
parameters.label: Align Translate Z
parameters.description: Sets the center of the geometry in Z for the align transform.

parameters.name: From Input
parameters.label: From Input
parameters.description: Determines which part of the input geometry to align to the Origin or Reference Input as selected in Post Translate parameter above.

parameters.name: To Reference
parameters.label: To Reference
parameters.description: Determines where on the reference the input is aligned.

parameters.name: Align Scale
parameters.label: Align Scale
parameters.description: Sets the scale of the geometry for the align transform.

parameters.name: Align Scale X
parameters.label: Align Scale X
parameters.description: If align scale is per axis, sets the X scale of the geometry for the align transform.

parameters.name: Align Scale Y
parameters.label: Align Scale Y
parameters.description: If align scale is per axis, sets the Y scale of the geometry for the align transform.

parameters.name: Align Scale Z
parameters.label: Align Scale Z
parameters.description: If align scale is per axis, sets the Z scale of the geometry for the align transform.

parameters.name: Mapping
parameters.label: Mapping
parameters.description: Start of Sequential Parameter Blocks for attribute-to-parameter mapping.

_provenance.fact: parameters and description scraped from the live Transform_POP wiki page ({{Parameter}}/{{ParameterItem}} templates)
